target.title: fix(fireworks): resolve catalog model params from plugin.json via core
target.body:
**TL;DR:** The two bundled Fireworks Kimi models silently ran with the wrong limits — a not-yet-configured `kimi-k2p6` resolved with the default template's 256,000 context window instead of its own 262,144, and `kimi-k2p5-turbo` could lose its `unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords` compat. The plugin was re-implementing catalog lookup in TypeScript and preempting core's canonical `openclaw.plugin.json` resolution. This PR deletes the parallel path and defers manifest ids to core. ## Summary **What does this fix?** `resolveFireworksDynamicModel` cloned the *default* template for any id not already in user config — flattening per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` declared in the manifest. Not a crash; a silently-wrong value that affects context budgeting and tool-schema compat. **What changed?** - The dynamic hook now returns `undefined` for manifest-declared ids, so core's bundled static catalog (`resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel`) resolves them from `openclaw.plugin.json` — one canonical, JSON-driven path. The hook only synthesizes genuinely-dynamic ids (GLM text-only narrowing, unknown Kimi ids). - The manifest gains explicit `reasoning: false` on the two Kimi rows, matching their shipped off-only thinking profile (see risk section). - Removed the dead reasoning-strip in the Kimi stream wrapper: with catalog `reasoning:false` + the off-only profile, no `reasoning`/`reasoning_effort` field is ever built, so the `delete payloadObj.reasoning*` lines were no-ops. The load-bearing `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` toggle stays (Fireworks Kimi leaks chain-of-thought into visible content without it). - `isFireworksGlmModelId` moved to `model-id.ts`; catalog clones via `structuredClone` (deep, covers `compat`). **Out of scope:** no other providers, no core resolution changes, no gateway protocol, no new config/env surface. ## Linked context - **#92217 (same author) is the complementary companion**: it adds four Fireworks reasoning models (DeepSeek V4 Pro, MiniMax M2.7, GLM-5.1, GPT-OSS 120B) with `reasoning_effort` compat and per-family thinking profiles. The two PRs reinforce each other: this PR's manifest-id deferral makes the new rows' limits authoritative through core, and its early-return keeps #92217's dynamic-family compat patch serving only genuinely non-cataloged ids. They conflict textually in 3 files (`index.ts`/`model-id.ts`/`provider-catalog.ts`) where both made near-identical changes (same GLM-helper move, same `structuredClone` direction); whichever lands second rebases mechanically. - Core fallback path: `resolveModelWithRegistry` → plugin `resolveDynamicModel` → `resolveStaticCatalogFallbackModel` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.ts`), gated by `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`. ## Real behavior proof Behavior or issue addressed: bundled Fireworks Kimi models apply their per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` from `openclaw.plugin.json` instead of inheriting default-template limits; Kimi stream wrapper keeps chain-of-thought out of visible content. Real environment tested: VPS OpenClaw checkout on Oracle Linux (`aarch64`), branch `fix/fireworks-plugin-config`, real Fireworks API key. Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --json` (and the `kimi-k2p5-turbo` equivalent); then live runs: `openclaw capability model run --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --prompt "Reply with exactly: ok" --thinking off --json` and the same with `--thinking high`. Evidence after fix: terminal capture and screenshot below. `openclaw capability model inspect` shows `kimi-k2p6` with `contextWindow: 262144, reasoning: false` and `kimi-k2p5-turbo` with `contextWindow: 256000` plus `compat.unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords: ["not"]`; both live `openclaw capability model run` calls (thinking off and high) returned this copied live output with no `…` leak: ```json { "ok": true, "capability": "model.run", "transport": "local", "provider": "fireworks", "model": "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6", "outputs": [{ "text": "ok", "mediaUrl": null }] } ``` Observed result after fix: each model surfaces its own distinct limits — the default template no longer flattens catalog metadata. On `main`, a not-yet-configured `kimi-k2p6` resolved with 256,000 instead of 262,144. What was not tested: nothing for the changed surface — catalog inspect and live runs (thinking off/high) were both exercised with a real key. Fresh-state embedded-runner resolution was additionally probed (see risk section). ## Tests and validation - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts`: 109 passed, incl. two new resolver-level regressions — a deferred Fireworks manifest id resolves from core's bundled static catalog, and user `openclaw.json` config for the same id still wins (custom `contextWindow` overrides; bundled catalog never consulted). Locks the precedence chain used by every neighbor provider. - `extensions/fireworks` vitest: 13 passed — new "defers manifest catalog models to core static-catalog resolution" test; per-model limits locked from the manifest; off-only thinking-profile coverage retained; the test that only covered the deleted reasoning-strip was removed with it. - `check:test-types`, `tsgo:extensions(+test)`, oxlint, oxfmt: clean. - Autoreview (claude sonnet-4-6, medium): "patch is correct (0.93), no accepted/actionable findings". ## Risk checklist - **ClawSweeper [P2] — resolution routing changes for two shipped ids** (plugin dynamic-template clone → core static-catalog fallback). Addressed three ways: (1) onboarded/configured installs resolve these ids from the model registry (generated from the same manifest) and never reach either fallback; (2) the entry points that can see a bare manifest id without registry coverage enable `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` (`runtime-llm.runtime.ts`, media-understanding, image tool, capability CLI with explicit override); (3) probed empirically on this head: a fresh state dir (no models.json, only the env API key) plus an embedded `openclaw agent --local` turn on deferred `kimi-k2p6` — the path where `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` is conditional — resolves and answers (`READY`). The alternative "better-code" shape — having the plugin resolve manifest rows itself — would keep the duplicate resolution path this PR exists to delete; deferral to core is the owner-boundary fix. - **ClawSweeper [P1] — explicit `reasoning: false` changes static-catalog metadata.** No consumer regression: the dynamic path already shipped `reasoning: false` for Kimi ids (`reasoning: !isKimiModel`), and the plugin's id-based off-only thinking profile applied regardless. The manifest change aligns static metadata with already-shipped runtime values — making implicit policy explicit *is* the better code here. - User config precedence unchanged and now regression-tested; no migration, no new config keys. - Net non-test LOC down (Source +1 overall per ClawSweeper; the rest is tests). ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review — specifically the bot's "accept the static-catalog handoff" decision (its recommended option 1). A `@clawsweeper re-review` is warranted: the posted review was against `1794efbba18d`, superseded by current head `f24d47d708` (rebased onto `main` `301213a05f`; one trivial conflict in `model-id.ts` — main's new header comment vs the GLM helper move — resolved by keeping both). What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Maintainer: provider/runtime-owner acceptance of the resolution-order handoff + ordering vs #92217 (whichever lands second rebases 3 files mechanically). Author/CI/external: nothing outstanding. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Both ClawSweeper risks are answered in the Risk checklist above with code-level analysis: [P2] via the registry/entry-point/fresh-state-probe argument (its option 2 "guard coverage" is satisfied by the new `model.test.ts` regressions plus the live probe), [P1] via the already-shipped-runtime-value argument. The bot's rank-up move (owner confirmation of the handoff) is the open item — that is a maintainer decision, not a code change.
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.
target.author: obuchowski

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
